directions.reason: Pupils sit high under raised eyelids and the chin lifts; the braid/crown is more prominent.
The upward eye line gains a small screen-right head turn from the 000 pose.
Labeled normal-size loop reads up-right, though blind horizontal review classified this intermediate pose left; accepted via qa/blind-review-resolution.json.
The head advances smoothly toward the right cardinal with a remaining upward eye cue.
Nose tip, pupils, and visible face plane clearly point to viewer screen-right.

The head and eyelids continue down-right in an even step; the saber stays attached.
A final continuous down-right step lands naturally before the down anchor.
Eyes are obscured by lowered lids and the chin points down distinctly.
The lowered gaze turns gently toward screen-left without a body or prop jump.
The face plane continues left with the chin still lowered.
A further continuous leftward turn retains a readable downward eyelid cue.
Nose tip, pupils, and visible face plane clearly orient to viewer screen-left.
